title: Code 20 No Forward or Reverse — Hydraulics Work | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forum discussion about fault code 20 where machine shows code 20 error with no forward or reverse drive but hydraulics (lift) still work. Code 20 on scissor lift platforms corresponds to traction motor controller fault (TM1 alarm). Diagnosis: check CAN bus communication between ECU and drive controller, inspect wiring harness between ECU and TM1 controller, verify motor controller power supply. On Dingli JCPT models code 20 = General Alarm TM1 indicating traction motor 1 controller fault or motor driver issue. Drive function disabled while lift hydraulics may remain operational. Solutions: inspect TM1 controller connections for corrosion, check motor phase wiring, test motor resistance, replace TM1 controller if wiring tests OK.

title: Genie GS2632 Error Codes C023 and C052 | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forum discussion about Genie GS-2632 scissor lift error codes C023 and C052. These codes are part of the Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS). C023 and C052 are DTC codes on the GS-2632 mid-size scissor lift (26ft working height, 32in deck). C023 relates to a drive circuit fault; C052 relates to a steering or brake coil fault. Community diagnosis includes: checking coil resistance (target 12-15 ohms for solenoid coils), inspecting wiring harness at solenoid valve block, verifying controller output voltage, testing drive motor. Genie GS-2632 uses same GSDS system as GS-1932 and GS-3246. Reset: cycle key switch after repairing underlying fault. Full DTC list in Genie service manual part 96316 or 97385.

title: Slow Lift Code 29 | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum thread about scissor lift exhibiting slow lift speed accompanied by fault code 29. Code 29 typically indicates hydraulic pressure sensor fault, pump performance degradation, or relief valve pressure setting issue. Slow lift with fault code 29 may indicate: insufficient hydraulic pressure (blocked suction filter reducing pump flow), failing hydraulic pump, pressure sensor failure, or relief valve opening prematurely at low pressure. Community diagnosis steps: (1) Check hydraulic fluid level; (2) Test pressure at pump outlet test port — compare to rated pressure in service manual (typically 16-20 MPa); (3) Inspect suction filter for blockage; (4) Verify pressure relief valve setting; (5) Test pressure sensor calibration. Models referenced: JLG and Genie scissor lifts.

title: 2015 Genie GS1930 Error Code C021 | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about a 2015 Genie GS-1930 displaying error code C021. C021 on Genie GS-1930/1932 is a DTC indicating a drive motor circuit fault or drive controller communication issue. The GS-1930 uses 24V DC drive system. C021 may indicate: motor controller fault, motor winding fault, or CAN bus communication failure between ECMs. Diagnosis steps: (1) Inspect drive motor connector and wiring; (2) Test motor winding resistance; (3) Check battery voltage under drive load; (4) Inspect drive controller connections; (5) If wiring and motor test OK, replace drive controller. Community notes: C021 on 2015 model with GEN 6 ECU may differ from older GEN 5 interpretation. Refer to Genie service manual part 97385.

title: JLG Flash Code 3-3 Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Technical Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction technical forum thread dedicated to JLG flash code 3-3 diagnosis. Flash code 3-3 indicates a short circuit in an output driver — one of the most common JLG fault codes with 50+ possible troubleshooting paths. Systematic isolation: (1) Disconnect platform harness connector at base machine — if code clears, fault is on platform side; if persists, fault is on base machine. (2) Check solenoid valve coil resistance individually (each should be 20-30 ohms; open or short indicates bad coil). (3) Inspect harness for chafed wires causing shorts. (4) Check sensor wiring (tilt, load, position sensors). (5) If all external components test OK, controller may be the fault. Models affected: JLG 450AJ, 2646ES, 3246ES, 45E, and other JLG models using pre-CAN flash code system.

title: Scissor Lift Code LL — Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forum thread about scissor lift displaying code LL on the LED ground control display. Code LL on Dingli JCPT series scissor lifts appears when the Emergency Stop button is activated or a critical safety interlock is engaged. Per Dingli operators manuals: push in and pull out the red Emergency Stop button to reset the LL code. Code LL is not a numeric fault code but indicates the E-stop circuit is open. Diagnosis: (1) Locate and release all E-stop buttons on platform and ground controls; (2) Inspect E-stop button for damage or stuck position; (3) Check E-stop circuit wiring for open circuit; (4) Verify all safety interlock switches (tilt sensor, pothole guard, overload sensor). Thread includes Dingli JCPT-specific guidance and general scissor lift LL code diagnosis.

title: JLG 1930ES Scissor Lift Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum thread about JLG 1930ES scissor lift troubleshooting and fault codes. JLG 1930ES: electric 19ft scissor lift, 30in platform width, 500 lb capacity, 24V DC system. Common fault codes: pothole guard faults (flash code 2-5), load management faults (code 9-2/9-3), drive motor faults, battery faults. Flash code reading: observe LED on ground control box. Uses same flash code system as JLG 2646ES and 3246ES. Battery: four 6V batteries in series for 24V system; check individual cell specific gravity monthly. Common issues: drive not working after pothole guard activation (reset by cycling E-stop), slow drive speed (check battery charge), intermittent fault codes (inspect connector corrosion in ground control box). Community tips from field technicians.

title: Manitou 160ATJ 180ATJ Access Platform Operator Manual (547370 EN) | HireSafe Solutions
content_summary: Manitou access platform operators manual covering 160 ATJ and 180 ATJ models (document 547370 EN, 22/11/2006), hosted by HireSafe Solutions UK. The 160ATJ and 180ATJ share the same platform control system. Contents: machine specifications for both models, safety instructions, pre-operation inspection, operating procedures, fault/error indicator descriptions and actions, emergency descent procedure, maintenance schedule. Fault indicators: overload warning (reduce platform load), tilt warning (level machine on flat surface), low fuel/hydraulic fluid warnings, engine fault warning, safety system faults (boom limit switches). Emergency procedures: manual boom lowering in case of power failure. Maintenance intervals: hydraulic oil check weekly, engine oil check daily, lubrication points monthly. Primary operator reference for Manitou 180ATJ fault indicator system and operator-level troubleshooting — distinct from service manual with technician-level fault codes via MDS software.

title: JLG Material Handling Diagnostic Software | Mechnician (Jaltest)
content_summary: Mechnician page covering Jaltest diagnostic software for JLG scissor lifts and boom lifts. The Jaltest diagnostic system interfaces with JLG equipment via OBD/CAN diagnostic port to read and clear fault codes, access live sensor data, and program controller parameters. Covers JLG boom lifts (450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ) and scissor lifts (3246ES, 4669LE). Key capabilities: real-time DTC reading and clearing, active and historical fault log, live sensor data (boom angles, hydraulic pressures, temperatures), controller programming and calibration. Diagnostic port location: ground control box analyzer port. Alternative to JLG proprietary analyzer (Part 1001249695) using Jaltest universal construction equipment diagnostic hardware. Useful for technicians servicing multiple AWP brands who need one diagnostic tool.

title: JLG Boom Lift Troubleshooting Guide | Intella Liftparts
content_summary: JLG boom lift troubleshooting guide from Intella Liftparts, a specialist JLG parts supplier. Covers common fault scenarios on JLG articulating (450AJ, 520AJ) and telescopic boom lifts (860SJ, 1200SJ). Troubleshooting topics: reading CAN bus fault codes via ground control analyzer port, wiring harness inspection for shorts and open circuits, sensor replacement procedures (tilt, load, position), controller diagnosis and replacement. Common faults: no drive function (check drive controller faults, ground drive enable circuit), boom extension fault (check telescope valve solenoid resistance and spool movement), platform leveling fault (check platform level sensor and actuator), engine fault codes (ECT stage 1/2, oil pressure). Key diagnostic approach: start with DTC group having highest first digits, use JLG Knowledge Base at jlg.com/directaccess for model-specific corrective actions.

title: Dingli JCPT 0607 DCS — J4A Equipment | Just4Access
content_summary: Just4Access (J4A) product listing for Dingli JCPT 0607 DCS scissor lift, catalog number J4A 1430. Specifications: working height 6m/20ft, platform dimensions 2.73m x 1.2m, rated load 450kg, drive speed (lowered) 4.0 km/h, battery 24V/225Ah, charge time 8 hours. The DCS (Dual Cylinder Standard) variant uses canbus control system with two hydraulic cylinders. Differentiates from DCM (DC Motor controller) and DCE variants. For JCPT0607DCS fault codes: refer to operators manual (ManualsLib #1617028 page 31) covering codes 10/20/30/31/32/51/52/53/54/57/58/59/110/111/112. European J4A catalog reference for identifying spare parts. Helps identify JCPT0607 variant-specific part numbers for European market machines.

title: Emergency Troubleshooting Methods for Scissor Lifts | Bruddylift
content_summary: Emergency troubleshooting guide for scissor lifts covering: (1) Platform stuck elevated - manual emergency descent procedure using gravity lowering valve or manual pump. (2) Complete power failure - locate emergency lowering valve, typically red valve on pump unit. (3) Drive failure - check battery charge, fuses, E-stop circuit, footswitch engagement. (4) Lift not raising - check hydraulic oil level, hydraulic pump motor connection, relief valve setting. (5) Machine will not start - verify all E-stops pulled out, footswitch depressed, key switch on, battery voltage above 23V (24V system). General procedure: (a) ensure safety, (b) check obvious causes (E-stop, battery), (c) review fault code display, (d) try manual reset (E-stop cycle), (e) consult manual. Applicable to Dingli, Genie, JLG, Haulotte scissor lifts.

title: Genie Lift Connect Troubleshooting Guide | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie Lift Connect troubleshooting guide from Genie (Terex). Genie Lift Connect is Genie's telematics/IoT fleet management system. Guide covers: (1) Connection troubleshooting - cellular/GPS module faults, SIM card issues, antenna checks. (2) Data sync problems - inspection data not uploading, connectivity verification steps. (3) Inspection instructions - pre-operation checklist integration with Lift Connect system. (4) Fault code integration - how Lift Connect captures and reports machine fault codes remotely. (5) Gateway device installation checks. Applicable to GS series scissor lifts, Z boom lifts, and S boom lifts with Lift Connect hardware installed. Official Genie manufacturer document (Terex/Genie).

title: How to understand and resolve a fault code — Haulotte Blog (Official)
content_summary: Official Haulotte guide on fault codes. All Haulotte fault codes start with letter 'F' followed by numbers/letters, e.g. F06.01 = load calibration must be carried out. Fault code categories: F01-F05 (electrical/battery), F06 (load management), F07 (sensor faults), F08-F09 (drive/steering), F10-F15 (CAN bus communication). Active (type A) failures appear on lower control display and warning light flickers on upper controls. Haulotte Diag app + VCI box (Vehicle Communication Interface) provides remote access to program, troubleshoot, calibrate all Haulotte machines — shows full diagnostic process including probable causes, resolution instructions, and real-time component status. ETI LITE: Haulotte's 24/7 technical search engine accessible by serial number. Fault codes remain uncleared until underlying issue is resolved. Contact Haulotte call-center for online troubleshooting support.

title: Troubleshoot solutions | Haulotte North America (Official)
content_summary: Official Haulotte North America troubleshooting page. Provides diagnostic support for all Haulotte AWP models including Optimum, Star, Compact, H-series boom lifts. Features fault code lookup, service manual access via ETI LITE portal, and Haulotte Diag software download. Common troubleshooting topics: machine will not start, fault code displayed, boom or scissor will not lift, drive malfunction, load management errors. Haulotte Diag application requires VCI wireless box for ECU connection. After-sales support: 1-855-693-4550. Models covered: Optimum 6/8, Star 6/8/10, Compact series, H12SX/H15SX/H18SX, HA12IP/HA15IP, 3522A, 3632T, Summit series.

title: Tech Tips | Haulotte North America (Official)
content_summary: Official Haulotte North America tech tips library. Contains specific technical service bulletins, repair tips, and fault resolution guides for Haulotte AWP equipment. Topics include: fault code CF07 motor temperature sensor diagnosis, load management calibration procedure (F06.01), battery charger fault codes, tilt sensor calibration, pothole guard adjustment, CAN bus troubleshooting for H-series boom lifts. ETI LITE serial-number-based document retrieval. Haulotte Diag VCI box pairing procedure. Pre-delivery inspection checklists. Tech tips are updated periodically with field service experience.

title: Failures List — Haulotte OPTIMUM 8 Training Manual Page 71 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte Optimum 8 scissor lift complete failures list from Training Manual (page 71). Failure code format: letter + 3-character code (e.g., CF07). CF07: Motor temperature sensor not correctly connected or damaged — check sensor wiring harness, replace sensor if open/short circuit. Active (type A) failures appear on lower control display; warning light flickers on upper controls. The Optimum 8 (also OPTIMUM 1931E) is an electric slab scissor lift with 8m working height, AC motor drive system. Fault codes are read from the CANBOX display unit. Reset procedure: cycle key switch after correcting fault. Haulotte Diag software provides full code descriptions and guided repair procedure for each fault code on all Optimum models.

title: Haulotte OPTIMUM 8 Maintenance Book Page 218 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte Optimum 8 maintenance book page 218 — maintenance and diagnostic information. The Optimum 8 maintenance book covers scheduled maintenance intervals, hydraulic system specifications, electrical system checks, and fault diagnosis procedures. Maintenance intervals: 250h, 500h, 1000h, 2000h. Hydraulic oil: ISO VG 46. Battery maintenance: check electrolyte level, charge state indicator. Fault code access via CANBOX display. Serial number location: frame right side. Covers models: Optimum 6E/6P, Optimum 8E, OPTIMUM 1331E, OPTIMUM 1931E.

title: Error Code Definitions — Haulotte Group 3522A Operator's Maintenance Manual Page 56 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte Group 3522A scissor lift error code definitions (Table 4-3). Fault code format: F followed by numbers. Control fault codes: F-04 load cell fault (check load sensor wiring); F-05 tilt sensor fault (machine tilted beyond limit — move to level ground); F-06 battery charger fault (see Table 4-1 battery charger codes); F-08 hydraulic over-temperature; F-10 throttle down fault (engine throttle control). Battery charger fault codes (Table 4-1): BCF-1 charger disconnected; BCF-2 battery overtemperature; BCF-3 excessive charge time. The 3522A is a 35ft articulating boom lift with diesel engine. Fault codes displayed on platform LED panel and ground control panel.

title: Error Code Definitions — Haulotte SUMMIT SERIES Operator's Manual Page 62 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte Summit Series scissor lift error code definitions (page 62). Summit Series includes HS15 SXPRO, HS18 SXPRO, HS21 SXPRO. Error codes displayed on CANBOX: F01-04 (battery/electrical faults), F05 (tilt sensor), F06-08 (load management), F10-15 (drive motor faults), F20-25 (CAN bus communication). F06.01: Load calibration required — press calibration button at ground control. F10: Drive motor controller fault — check motor controller wiring. F15: CAN communication timeout — check CAN bus wiring and termination resistors. Reset: cycle key switch. Haulotte Diag VCI box required for advanced diagnostics.

title: Haulotte Star 10 Operator's Manual | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte Star 10 scissor lift operator's manual index page. The Star 10 is a 10m (33ft) rough terrain scissor lift with diesel engine. Manual covers: safety, operation, fault code interpretation, emergency procedures, maintenance schedule. Star 10 fault codes start with F and are displayed on CANBOX. Common Star 10 fault codes from related Star 8/10 training manual: CF07 motor temperature sensor, F05 tilt fault, F06 load calibration. Star 8/10 service manual (Scribd document 438668382) covers: wiring diagrams for CANBOX system, hydraulic schematic, drive motor circuit (24V DC), scissor mechanism adjustment. Manual revision: 2022 MU STAR10 AE 36A.

title: Haulotte H12 SX Maintenance Book | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte H12 SX maintenance book — covers H12 SX, H12 SXL, H15 SX, H15 SXL, H18 SX, H18 SXL, HS3388 RT, HS3388 RT XL, HS4388 RT, HS5388 RT models. Slab scissor lift family with AC motor drive. Maintenance intervals: 250h, 500h, 1000h. Fault code system: CANBOX display, Haulotte Diag for advanced diagnostics. Battery system: 24V DC traction. Hydraulic system: reservoir capacity 15L, oil type ISO VG 46. Common faults: F05 tilt (rough terrain use check), F06 overload calibration, CF07 motor temperature. The H12/H15/H18 SX series uses same fault code structure as Optimum and Star series. Serial number determines which maintenance manual revision applies.
equipment_model: H12 SX / H15 SX / H18 SX

title: Battery Fault Codes — Haulotte Group 3632T Operator's Maintenance Manual Page 39 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte 3632T scissor lift battery charger fault codes (Table 4-1, page 39). Battery charger fault codes: BCF-1 Charger disconnected or AC power lost — check AC power supply, charger cable connection; BCF-2 Battery overtemperature — allow battery to cool, check ventilation; BCF-3 Excessive charge time — battery may be sulfated, replace battery pack; BCF-4 Charger internal fault — replace charger unit; BCF-5 Low battery voltage at start of charge — deeply discharged, may not recover. General fault codes F-03 through F-10 also listed. The 3632T is a 36ft tall rough terrain scissor lift with 3200 lb platform capacity. Fault codes accessible from ground control display.

title: Haulotte Star 8 / Star 10 Service Repair Manual PDF | Scribd
content_summary: Haulotte Star 8 and Star 10 rough terrain scissor lift service repair manual (PDF). Covers: safety specifications, technical characteristics, hydraulic system pressures and diagrams, electrical wiring diagrams, CANBOX fault code system, tightening torques, adjustment procedures, component replacement. Fault code system: CANBOX controller reads sensor inputs and generates F-series codes. CF07 motor temperature fault: check motor thermistor connector at motor terminal block. Hydraulic system: lift cylinder, drive motor circuit, relief valve settings. Diesel engine faults handled separately by engine ECU (separate fault code system from machine controller codes).

title: Skyjack Technical Publications — Official Portal
content_summary: Official Skyjack Technical Publications portal — provides service manuals, parts manuals, operators manuals, and service bulletins for all Skyjack SJIII, SJIV, SJ 30 Series, SJ 40 Series scissor lifts, and SkyPower boom lifts. Models covered: SJIII 3219, 3220, 3226, 4620, 4626, 4632, SJIV 4740, SJ 3226, SJ 4740, SJ 6826, SJ 6832. Document search by model number or serial number. LED error code flash charts, wiring diagrams, hydraulic schematics, diagnostic flowcharts. Includes service bulletins for common field issues: GP102 controller faults, load sensing calibration, hydraulic drift correction.

title: LED Error Codes Quick Reference Chart — Skyjack SJIII 3226 Service Manual Page 117 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Skyjack SJIII 3226 LED error codes quick reference chart (service manual page 117). Error codes displayed by LED flash sequences and alphanumeric readout on GP102 controller. Codes include: IN GROUND MOD (machine in ground mode, platform stowed); OV RLOAD FUNCTIONS DISABL D (overload detected, functions disabled — remove excess load); ARMGUARD ACTIV (arm guard/pothole guard activated); IDL TIM OUT ACTIV (idle timeout, restart required); NO LAST CALDAT (no calibration data stored — recalibrate load sensor); DRIV/LIFT INPUTS FAULTY (drive or lift joystick input fault — check joystick wiring); MS INPUTS FAULTY (motion supervisor input fault); P3-4 SHORT TO SUPPLY (wiring short circuit between pins 3-4); HWFS STALL D (hardware fault safety stall detected). GP102 reports one error per instance. First thing to check: two manually operated valves (drive enable and lift enable) that can block operation. Reset: cycle key switch.

title: Skyjack SJIII 4626 Parts Manual (Rentalex PDF)
content_summary: Skyjack SJIII 4626 maintenance and parts manual covering models 3220, 3226, 4620, 4626 (Conventionals). Parts breakdown includes: GP102 controller assembly, drive motor/pump assembly, traction motor parts, steering cylinder, load sensor, platform control box, chassis wiring harness, hydraulic manifold assembly. Key part numbers for fault-related components: GP102 controller module, load cell sensor, tilt sensor, platform joystick assembly. Troubleshooting section covers: LED error code interpretation, hydraulic system checks, drive motor testing procedure (resistance check: forward 2-4Ω, reverse 2-4Ω), pothole guard sensor adjustment. The SJIII 4626 has 26ft platform height, 1000 lb capacity, 48V DC electric system.

title: Skyjack SJIII Conventional Operators Manual — Models 3015, 3219, 3220, 3226, 4620, 4626 (Rentalex PDF)
content_summary: Skyjack SJIII Conventionals and Compacts operators manual covering models 3015, 3219, 3220, 3226, 4620, 4626. Operation indicator codes (OIC) displayed on LED: normal operation indicators vs. fault indicators. Fault indicator sequence: LED flashes in sets — first set = fault category, second set = specific fault number (same format as JLG flash codes). Emergency lowering procedure when fault active: release E-stop, use ground lowering valve. Common operator-induced faults: tilt sensor active (uneven surface), overload (too much weight), pothole guard activated (driving over edge), idle timeout (machine parked too long). Battery indicator: 5-bar gauge, operation disabled below 20% charge. Models use 24V or 48V DC systems depending on year and model variant.

title: Common Scissor Lift Fault Codes and Quick Fixes — Genie & Skyjack Guide (Nam Rentals 2026)
content_summary: Comprehensive fault code guide covering Genie GS series and Skyjack SJIII series scissor lifts (published February 2026). Genie fault codes: E1 (Emergency Stop circuit open — release and reset E-stop), E4 (Low battery — charge before operating), E6 (Tilt sensor active — move to level ground), E12/E13 (Control communication fault — check CAN wiring), DTC 18 (Pothole guard not deploying — inspect guard mechanism), DTC 44-47 (Coil fault — check solenoid coil resistance 20-30Ω). Skyjack SJIII fault codes: OVRLOAD (overload — reduce load), ARMGRD (arm guard/pothole guard), DRIVFLT (drive fault — check motor and wiring), TILT (tilt sensor — level machine), LOWBATT (low battery — charge), HWFLT (hardware fault — call service). Quick fixes: always check E-stop first, verify battery charge, inspect for visible damage before calling service.

title: Skyjack SJIII 4632 Maintenance Manual PDF | Scribd
content_summary: Skyjack SJIII Series maintenance manual for model SJIII 4632 (DC Electric Scissors, 223198ABA August 2020, ANSI/CSA/CE/AS/KC). Covers: 4740 and 4632 model troubleshooting. LED error codes, hydraulic system maintenance, traction motor testing, load sensor calibration procedure, pothole guard adjustment and testing. SJIII 4632 specifications: 32ft platform height, 1500 lb capacity, 48V DC drive system. Fault code system: GP102 controller generates alphanumeric codes. Service Manual part number 223198ABA.

title: Fault Codes — Snorkel SL26 Plus / SL30 User Manual Page 20 | ManualsDir
content_summary: Snorkel SL26 Plus and SL30 scissor lift fault code list (user manual page 20, serial number SN51000+). Fault codes and descriptions for DC electric scissor lifts. Common fault codes: NEUTRAL FAULT — control system detects lift not in neutral position, preventing movement (check neutral safety switch/sensor, inspect wiring harness for damage); OVERLOAD — platform load exceeds rated capacity (reduce load, check load sensor); TILT — machine on uneven surface (drive to level ground, check tilt sensor); BATTERY LOW — charge below operating threshold (charge immediately); DRIVE FAULT — drive motor circuit fault (check motor wiring and controller). Emergency lowering: open emergency lowering valve at base of cylinder. Reset procedure: push and release E-stop button. The SL26 Plus has 26ft platform height, 500 lb capacity. The SL30 has 30ft platform height.

title: Troubleshooting Chart — Snorkel SL20 Operator's Manual Page 44 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Snorkel SL20 scissor lift troubleshooting chart (operator's manual chapter 12, page 44). Troubleshooting table organized by symptom: Machine will not drive — check E-stop, battery charge, drive enable switch, motor controller fault indicator; Machine will not lift — check hydraulic fluid level, pump fuse, lift solenoid valve resistance (20-30Ω), overload sensor; Machine drifts down when elevated — check lift cylinder seals, holding valve, solenoid valve O-rings; Fault indicator LED on — read flash code sequence, note first/second flash count for specific diagnosis; Horn sounds continuously — tilt sensor activated (machine on slope) or overload detected. The SL20 is a 20ft electric slab scissor lift. Similar troubleshooting applies to SL26 and SL30 models as they share the same control architecture.

title: Battery Charge Fault Code — Snorkel SL26RTE Parts & Service Manual Page 110 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Snorkel SL26RTE rough terrain electric scissor lift battery charge fault codes (service manual page 110). Battery charger fault indicators on Snorkel SL26RTE: Fault 1 — AC power disconnected during charge cycle; Fault 2 — Battery temperature too high (>45°C), charging suspended; Fault 3 — Charge timeout exceeded (battery may be faulty or deeply discharged); Fault 4 — Charger internal fault (replace charger); Fault 5 — Battery voltage too low to accept charge (battery damaged/sulfated). The SL26RTE is a rough terrain variant of the SL26 with foam-filled tires and 4WD. Battery system: 24V DC with on-board charger. Fault code displayed by LED on charger module. Note: disconnecting battery while fault code is active clears non-latching codes; latching codes require code-specific repair.

title: Snorkel SL26/30 Speed Level Series Service & Parts Manual (snorkeljp.com)
content_summary: Snorkel SL26/SL30 Speed Level Series self-propelled scissor lift service and parts manual (serial numbers 50001+). Service manual covers: fault code list on page 15, hydraulic system troubleshooting, electrical system wiring diagrams, motor controller specifications, battery maintenance. Fault code access: LED indicator on control module flashes coded sequences. Drive system: 24V DC traction motors. Hydraulic system: 12V pump motor, manual descent valve. Load sensing system: load cell with calibration procedure. Parts list includes all serviceable components with Snorkel part numbers. Emergency procedures: manual lowering, battery disconnect.

title: Snorkel Lift Support — Official Snorkel Support Page
content_summary: Official Snorkel support page providing technical resources for Snorkel aerial work platforms (AWP/MEWP). Resources available: service manuals, operator manuals, parts manuals, wiring diagrams for SL series (SL20, SL26, SL26RTE, SL26 Plus, SL30), TB series telescopic booms, and AB series articulating booms. Technical support contact: +1-800-678-7493. Models covered: SL-series (electric slab and rough terrain scissor lifts), TB series, AB series. Fault code troubleshooting: contact dealer network or Snorkel technical support for model-specific fault code interpretation. Snorkel uses a simple LED flash code system — number of flashes indicates fault category.
equipment_model: SL20 / SL26 / SL30 / TB series / AB series

title: Troubleshooting Guide — Upright MX19 Service and Parts Manual Page 47 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Upright MX19 electric scissor lift troubleshooting guide (service and parts manual page 47). Troubleshooting table by symptom: No drive in either direction but lift works — check drive motor and hydraulic supply, inspect chassis control system for electrical faults or blown fuses; Lift solenoid fault — inspect solenoid wiring, resistance should be 20-30Ω; Right/left steering solenoid fault — check wiring and resistance; Overload — remove excess load, check load sensor calibration; Brake release switch engaged or open — adjust brake release switch. Motor controller replacement: cycle power before replacing controller (fault may clear on power cycle). The MX19 is a 19ft electric scissor lift with 24V DC drive system. Similar fault code system to UL series.

title: Upright MX19 Service and Parts Manual | ManualsLib
content_summary: Upright MX19 electric scissor lift complete service and parts manual index page. The MX19 (19ft, 800 lb capacity, 24V DC) manual covers: specifications, hydraulic system, electrical system, troubleshooting guide, parts list. MX19 fault code system: fault indicator LED on control module, fault codes read by counting LED flash sequences. Common MX19 faults: no drive (drive motor/controller fault), no lift (pump motor fault or hydraulic low), beeping alarm (overload or tilt). Manual also covers related models: MX15, MX19 ST (Slab-Terrain). Upright brand now part of Snorkel International — manuals may reference both brand names. Download: UPRIGHT MX19 SERVICE AND PARTS MANUAL PDF available on ManualsLib.

title: Upright MX19 — Technical Troubleshooting Discussion | Forkliftaction
content_summary: Forkliftaction.com forum thread on Upright MX19 scissor lift troubleshooting. Forum discusses common MX19 faults: drive motor failure, hydraulic control valve malfunction, drive solenoid issues. When MX19 will not drive forward or reverse but lift and steer work — check drive motor hydraulic supply first (common issue: drive valve stuck or blocked), then inspect chassis control system for electrical faults or blown fuses affecting drive functions. Also discussed: Upright MX19 wiring diagram access, fault code interpretation, service manual availability. Community members note MX19 uses same control system architecture as Snorkel SL series after Upright was acquired by Snorkel. Score 0.8 — community forum with verified technician responses.

title: Upright MX19 Scissor Lift — Heavy Equipment Forums
content_summary: Heavy Equipment Forums discussion thread on Upright MX19 scissor lift issues and troubleshooting. Topics covered: MX19 won't move — check manual lowering valve is fully closed, check E-stop buttons at both ground and platform, verify interlock switches (platform gate switch, drive enable switch). MX19 beeping alarm: tilt sensor activated (uneven surface) or overload condition. Hydraulic drift (platform creeping down): check cylinder seals and holding valve. Forum members discuss: fault LED blink codes, finding replacement parts (Upright/Snorkel part numbers), alternative motor controller sources. The MX19 was produced from mid-1990s through early 2000s — older machines may have controller compatibility issues.

title: Common Genie DC Slab Scissor Lift Fault Codes | Aerial Equipment Parts
content_summary: Guide to common Genie DC slab scissor lift fault codes covering GS-1930DC, GS-1932DC, GS-2032DC, GS-2632DC, GS-3232DC models. DC slab scissor faults: E1 Emergency Stop — release and reset E-stop on both platform and ground controls; E4 Low Battery — recharge (battery below 20%); E6 Tilt — machine on slope exceeding 3°, move to level surface; E12 Platform communication fault — check CAN wiring between platform and ground box; DTC 42 Forward drive coil fault — check wiring, resistance 20-30Ω; DTC 43 Reverse drive coil fault; DTC 44 Up coil fault; DTC 45 Down coil fault; DTC 52-58 (various coil faults — check individual solenoid resistance); DTC 68 Low voltage shutdown. DC slab models use same fault code structure as AC models but all solenoid coils are DC powered. Reset: cycle power via key switch.

title: Electric Scissor Lift Won't Start — Practical Troubleshooting Guide (March 2026)
content_summary: Comprehensive troubleshooting guide for electric scissor lifts that will not start (updated March 2026). Diagnostic sequence: (1) Check all E-stop buttons (platform and ground) — push and release to reset; (2) Verify key switch in ON position; (3) Check battery voltage (should be >22V for 24V systems, >44V for 48V systems); (4) Check main fuse and contactor fuse; (5) Check drive enable switch and platform gate switch (interlock circuit); (6) Look for fault code on display. Common causes by brand: Genie — E4 (low battery) or E1 (E-stop), JLG — flash code series (read both flashes), Dingli — code 69 (power relay sleep), Skyjack — LED code display. If fault code shown: cross-reference with service manual. If no code but machine dead: check main contactor (should click when key ON), check charger function. Applicable to: Genie GS series, JLG 3246ES, Dingli JCPT series, Skyjack SJIII series, Snorkel SL series.

title: JLG 1350 Boom Lift Error Code 848 Troubleshooting | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 1350SJP boom lift error code 848 troubleshooting discussion (JustAnswer). Error code 848 on JLG telescopic boom lifts typically indicates a CAN bus communication fault between the ground module (GM) and the platform module (PM). Troubleshooting steps for JLG 848: (1) Check CAN bus wiring continuity from ground to boom tip — look for damaged wires in boom track; (2) Verify CAN termination resistors at each end of bus (120Ω); (3) Check ground module and platform module power supply; (4) Use JLG Analyzer (part 1001249695) to read live CAN bus status; (5) If wiring OK, test GM/PM module boards. The 1350SJP is a 135ft telescopic boom lift. Similar fault applies to 860SJ, 1000SJP, 1200SJP with same CAN architecture.

title: Haulotte Group H15SX Manuals Index | ManualsLib

title: Skyjack Lift 3219 — Common Errors & Quick Fixes | Hugg & Hall
content_summary: Common error codes and quick fixes for Skyjack SJIII 3219 electric scissor lift, published by equipment rental company Hugg & Hall. The SJIII 3219 uses GP102 controller with alphanumeric LED. Basic reset: Emergency Stop push-in and release. Quick reference for operators and non-specialist technicians.

title: Skyjack SJIII BDE Troubleshooting — Section 4 Troubleshooting Information

Error code isolation method: divide system (electrical vs hydraulic) to locate fault. Applicable to SJIII BDE models including 3215, 3219, 3226 with battery-electric drive.

title: DEUTZ DTC-Codes EMR5 — Diagnosis and Error Codes DTC-Code SPN FMI | Official Deutz Serdia
content_summary: Official Deutz EMR5 engine diagnostic trouble codes (DTC) with SPN (Suspect Parameter Number) and FMI (Failure Mode Identifier) values. Published on Deutz Serdia official portal. FMI meanings: 0=above normal, 1=below normal, 2=erratic data, 3=voltage high, 4=voltage low, 5=open circuit, 12=bad device. Key AWP-relevant codes: coolant temperature warning (reduce engine load), rail pressure faults (fuel system), oil pressure low (engine shutdown), exhaust temperature sensor. Category DE/EN bilingual document.

Fault code procedure for codes 51-69: check fault code list to identify problem component (replacement required, cannot be reset). A38E fault code ranges: 01-39 = control/drive system faults (resettable); 51-69 = safety system faults (component replacement required). SN004500 and later models.

title: Snorkel TB Boom Lift Service Manual (TB42 and TB Series) | Scribd
content_summary: Snorkel TB-series boom lift service manual on Scribd, covering TB42 and related Deutz-powered telescopic boom models. Common TB42 fault diagnosis: (1) Neutral fault — control system detects not-in-neutral, prevents movement (check neutral safety switch/sensor on transmission or control module for fault or misalignment); (2) Drive shift fault — TB42 remains in low gear, cannot shift to high (verify all shift solenoids for activation, check switch and wiring harness for continuity and corrosion); (3) Hydraulic system issues — after component replacement, verify circuit breakers for power, inspect hydraulic pump drive for mechanical faults; (4) Engine starting — ignition coil failure or CDI unit failure causes no-spark condition. Hydraulic system: open-circuit hydraulic with Deutz diesel engine direct drive.

title: Skyjack SJ45T Telescopic Boom Lift Operating Manual

Emergency descent: manual lowering valve location included. Reset procedure: key switch off, resolve condition, restart. Applicable to SJ45T models in Australian and European markets.

title: Skyjack Tech Tip — First Thing To Check When Fault Code Appears (2015/2021) | Skyjack Official
content_summary: Official Skyjack tech tip document (2015, republished March 2021) — first diagnostic steps when fault code appears on Skyjack equipment. Systematic approach before component replacement: (1) Verify all safety switches in normal positions (platform gate closed, footswitch released, E-stop pulled out); (2) Confirm machine is on level ground (tilt sensor must not be active — max 2.5° slope for most SJIII models); (3) Check platform load is within rated capacity; (4) Verify battery charge level and charger connection; (5) Inspect pothole guard deployment (both sides must be fully down). Basic reset: Emergency Stop push in and pull out. Key message: most fault codes are resolved by addressing basic conditions, not component replacement. Applies to SJIII scissor lifts and SJ boom lifts.

title: Deutz EMR3 Engine Diagnostic Trouble Codes | DTC Reference
content_summary: Deutz EMR3 engine management system diagnostic trouble codes reference (Deutz Service Information Systems). Contains SPN/FMI code table for EMR3. FMI failure mode categories: 0=data valid but above normal, 1=data valid but below normal, 2=data erratic/intermittent/incorrect, 3=voltage above normal, 4=voltage below normal, 5=current below normal (open circuit), 12=bad intelligent device. Key AWP fault codes: coolant temperature stage 1 (warning only, reduce load), coolant temperature stage 2 (engine shutdown imminent), fuel pressure below normal, crankshaft sensor failure. Used by Deutz dealers and AWP technicians to decode engine-side faults separate from machine platform control faults.

title: Error 6 - Maintenance & Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about Error 6 on aerial work platforms (multiple JLG models). Error 6 typically indicates tilt sensor activation: machine detects ground inclination beyond safe operating limits. Diagnostic steps from forum: (1) Verify machine is on level ground — move to flat surface. (2) Check tilt sensor wiring and connector integrity. (3) Inspect tilt sensor for physical damage or contamination. (4) Verify tilt sensor calibration — may need recalibration after replacement. (5) If Error 6 appears on flat ground: tilt sensor likely faulty — test sensor output voltage, check resistance, replace if out of spec. Experienced technicians note that connector corrosion is common cause of intermittent Error 6 on older machines.

title: JLG 450AJ Series 1 Ford Engine - Hydraulic Loss of Control to Valves - Design & Engineering | Forkliftaction
content_summary: Forkliftaction Design & Engineering discussion on JLG 450AJ Series 1 (Ford engine) intermittent and total loss of hydraulic valve control — affects: platform rotate, platform leveling override, jib, telescope, tower lift, and steering valves. Core finding: connector terminal corrosion inside harness connectors between platform and tractor unit is primary cause of intermittent valve control loss on aged JLG 450AJ units. Recommended checks: inspect all harness connectors at platform/boom junction for internal terminal oxidation, check connector seating and locking, clean or replace corroded pins. JLG handset required to pull active fault codes from ECM. If codes clear but symptoms return, replace connector housings — intermittent internal corrosion evades visual inspection.

title: Snorkel Lift Neutral Fault - Won't Move | JustAnswer
content_summary: Snorkel scissor lift displaying NEUTRAL FAULT error — machine won't move in any direction. NEUTRAL FAULT means drive joystick is not returning to center position or neutral switch detecting off-center input at startup. Troubleshooting steps: (1) Check joystick for physical binding, debris, or mechanical damage. (2) Inspect joystick wiring and connector for damage/corrosion. (3) Test joystick voltage output — should read midpoint (2.5V on 5V systems) when released. (4) Check neutral switch/sensor continuity. (5) Perform joystick calibration in controller menu. (6) If calibration fails, replace joystick assembly. Snorkel S-series and SL-series: NEUTRAL FAULT most commonly caused by worn joystick potentiometer not returning to true center.

title: JLG Boom Lift Flash Code 2 Sets of 9 Flashes (9-9) Troubleshooting
content_summary: JLG boom lift displaying flash code 2 sets of 9 flashes (code 9-9 or 99). JLG flash code 9-9 indicates Power Module Failure or Personality Range Error — same as codes 9-9/99-5. The first number identifies the system group, second identifies specific fault. Code 9-9: the power module (PE) received an out-of-range personality value, indicating EPROM corruption. Troubleshooting: (1) Release all E-stops. (2) Cycle power completely (30 second minimum E-stop hold). (3) If 9-9 persists, connect JLG Analyzer for underlying DTC. (4) 9-9 confirmed = power module replacement or reprogramming via JLG service required. Affects: JLG ES Scissors (1930ES, 2630ES, 3246ES), articulating booms (450AJ, 520AJ), telescopic booms (860SJ).

title: Skyjack Lift Does Nothing - Complete Diagnostic Guide | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction community troubleshooting for Skyjack lift with no response when powered on. Expert community advice: (1) Verify all E-stops released — check both platform and ground control E-stop buttons. (2) Check battery voltage — Skyjack requires minimum 24V (or 48V for larger models). (3) Check LED display on ground control for alphanumeric fault code before further diagnosis. (4) Inspect ground strap connections — Skyjack grounding issues common cause of complete no-function. (5) Check main contactor and fuses. (6) If GP102 controller shows code: OVRLOAD (reduce load and restart), TILT (level ground), DRIVE FAULT (motor/controller fault). Emergency lowering: manual descent valve on hydraulic manifold.

title: Haulotte HA26 RTJ PRO SW Manuals Index | ManualsLib
content_summary: ManualsLib index page for Haulotte HA26 RTJ PRO SW articulating boom lift documentation. Lists all available manuals including Training Manual, Maintenance Book, and Operator's Manual. The HA26 RTJ PRO SW is Haulotte's large diesel articulating boom (26m working height). Uses same F-series fault code system as other Haulotte boom lifts (HA16, HA20): F01-F25 codes for hydraulic/electrical/sensor faults, BCF-1 through BCF-5 for battery charger faults. Haulotte Diag software with VCI interface required for full diagnostic access to controller-level codes. ETI LITE portal provides remote diagnostics. Contact: Haulotte North America haulotte-usa.com or Haulotte Community haulottecommunity.com for fault code library.

title: Haulotte HA26 RTJ PRO SW Training Manual | ManualsLib
content_summary: Haulotte HA26 RTJ PRO SW articulating boom lift training manual, full document on ManualsLib. Contains detailed fault code system explanation for the HA26 RTJ PRO SW. F-series fault codes (F01-F25): F01 = main contactor fault, F02 = motor drive fault, F03 = hydraulic pump motor fault, F04 = overload (check load cell and calibration), F05 = tilt sensor active (level machine), F06 = platform level sensor fault, F08 = emergency stop activated, F10 = engine fault (check Deutz ECU), F15 = CAN bus communication error, F25 = general controller fault. Diagnostic procedure: use Haulotte Diag software with VCI box, or read LED codes from control box. Training manual also covers: pre-operation checks, hydraulic system, drive troubleshooting, SPN/FMI Deutz engine codes integration. Reset: press Reset button on ground panel after correcting fault.

title: Snorkel Product Range Multifold Guide | Snorkel Official
content_summary: Official Snorkel product range multifold guide in English (PDF). Covers all Snorkel product lines: SL-series electric scissors, S-series electric scissors, A-series articulating booms, TB-series telescopic booms, and SB-series boom lifts. Snorkel's fault code architecture across product lines: SL/S-series scissors use numeric LED flash codes (01-69 range); A-series articulating booms use same numeric flash code format (01-39 = resettable, 51-69 = component replacement required); TB/SB boom lifts use Snorkel EMS system with dedicated display. Common cross-model codes: 01 = startup error check, 11 = drive fault, 21 = lift fault, 31 = steering fault (where applicable), 51 = solenoid fault (check coil resistance 20-30Ω), 61 = sensor fault. Contact Snorkel Support: snorkellifts.com/Snorkel-Support/
equipment_model: Snorkel SL-series, Snorkel A-series, Snorkel SB-series

title: Upright Scissor Lift Operator Manuals Library | Equipment Parts Plus
content_summary: Equipment Parts Plus documentation library for Upright scissor lift operator manuals. Covers all UpRight models: UL25 (25ft, DC electric), UL30 (30ft, DC electric), RT10 (rough terrain, 10ft), MX19 (19ft all-terrain electric), MX26 (26ft). Upright was rebranded as Snorkel in 2010 — Snorkel manuals also apply post-2010. UpRight/Snorkel scissor lifts use LED flash code system: codes 01-39 are operator-resettable faults (E-stop, drive fault, lift fault, tilt, pothole); codes 51-69 indicate component failure requiring technician (solenoid open/short circuit, sensor failure, controller fault). Solenoid coil test: measure resistance 20-30Ω; outside range = replace coil. Platform E-stop must be pulled out before any diagnostic attempt. Also hosts service bulletins for common field issues on UL/RT/MX series.
equipment_model: Upright UL25, Upright UL30, Upright RT10, Upright MX19

title: Upright X-Series Scissor Lift Service and Parts Manual (SN 6013-15019) | Snorkel Japan
content_summary: Upright X-Series scissor lift service and parts manual covering serial numbers 6013-15019, available from Snorkel Japan official portal. The X-Series covers electric slab scissor lifts. Service manual includes: complete fault code table for X-Series controller, hydraulic circuit diagram, electrical schematics with component locations, and parts list with part numbers. X-Series fault code format: numeric LED codes. Key codes: Code 01 = startup fault (check E-stop/footswitch), Code 11 = drive motor fault (check motor brushes, wiring), Code 21 = lift motor fault (check pump motor, check hydraulic fluid), Code 31 = tilt fault (level machine, check tilt sensor), Code 51 = drive solenoid open circuit (resistance should be 20-30Ω), Code 61 = lift solenoid fault. All codes 51-69 require component replacement. Reset procedure: correct fault, cycle key switch.

title: Deutz Engine DTC/SPN Fault Code List | Deutz Technical Reference
content_summary: Comprehensive Deutz engine diagnostic trouble code (DTC) and SPN/FMI fault code reference list. Covers EMR2, EMR3, EMR4, EMR5, and MD1 controller generations. Key DTC categories: SPN 100 (Engine Oil Pressure — low oil pressure, check level and sensor), SPN 110 (Engine Coolant Temperature — overheating, check coolant level, radiator), SPN 157 (Fuel Rail Pressure — check fuel filter, injection pump), SPN 190 (Engine Speed — flywheel sensor fault), SPN 91 (Throttle Position — check accelerator pedal/sensor), SPN 636 (Crankshaft Position), SPN 637 (Camshaft Position). FMI codes: FMI 3 = voltage high/open circuit, FMI 4 = voltage low/short to ground, FMI 5 = current low/open circuit, FMI 12 = failed component, FMI 14 = special instruction. Relevant for: JLG 510AJ/520AJ (Deutz D2.9L4), Manitou 180ATJ (Deutz TCD3.6), Haulotte HA20/HA26 RTJ, Skyjack SJ46AJ+ diesel booms.
equipment_model: Deutz EMR2, Deutz EMR3, Deutz MD1

title: Deutz TCD3.6 Engine Fault Codes - Genie GTH-844 Service Manual Page 78 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GTH-844 telehandler service and repair manual page 78 containing Deutz TCD3.6 engine fault codes. The TCD3.6 engine is used in multiple AWP brands including Manitou 180ATJ. Fault codes from this page (SPN/FMI format): SPN 100 FMI 1 = Oil Pressure Critically Low (shutdown), SPN 100 FMI 17 = Oil Pressure Warning, SPN 110 FMI 15 = Coolant Temperature High Warning, SPN 110 FMI 0 = Coolant Temperature Critically High (shutdown), SPN 157 FMI 1 = Fuel Rail Pressure Low, SPN 190 FMI 14 = Engine Overspeed Protection Active, SPN 91 FMI 3/4 = Accelerator Pedal Position Sensor Open/Short. Corrective actions: check sensor wiring/connectors first before replacing components; oil pressure faults require oil level check and filter inspection; coolant faults require radiator inspection and leak test. Also applicable to: Haulotte diesel boom lifts with TCD3.6, JLG diesel platforms with Deutz engines.

title: JLG X550AJ Electric Articulating Boom Lift Operation and Safety Manual | JLG Official
content_summary: Official JLG operation and safety manual for the X550AJ electric articulating boom lift (compact crawler boom). The X550AJ is an electric boom lift featuring JLG's advanced control system with on-board DTC display. Fault codes appear on the machine display and can also be read via JLG Analyzer. Key electrical boom fault indicators: E-stop status, battery charge level, tilt sensor, drive fault, platform overload. JLG X550AJ specific codes: Machine Tilt (tilt sensor active — level machine), Overload (reduce load and recalibrate), Drive Enable (footswitch or E-stop issue), Battery Low (charge batteries — minimum 80% for operation). As an electric boom, also includes: battery voltage monitoring, motor controller temperature codes, regenerative braking fault codes. Manual covers pre-operation inspection, operating procedures, and emergency lowering procedures.

title: Genie GS-2669/3369/4069 BE Section 5 Fault Codes | Manualzz (P/N 1257106)
content_summary: Genie GS-2669 BE, GS-3369 BE, GS-4069 BE (Battery Electric) scissor lifts fault codes — Section 5 of service manual P/N 1257106. The GS-4069 BE is a 13.7m working-height DC electric scissor. Fault code architecture: same GSDS (Genie SmartLink Diagnostic Systems) used across GS series. DTC types: H (hydraulic), P (power), U (user interface), F (sensors), C (controls). OIC codes: LL (tilt), PHS (high-position override), E1 (E-stop), E4 (low battery), E6 (tilt sensor), E12/E13 (CAN communication). The BE variants use electric drive motors with similar code sets to GS-1930/1932 but scaled for larger platform capacity. 272 DTCs total across GSDS.
equipment_model: GS-4069 BE / GS-3369 BE / GS-2669 BE

title: Genie GS-2669 DC Service and Repair Manual P/N 1272220 | Genie Official
content_summary: Official Genie service and repair manual for GS-2669 DC (rough terrain DC-powered scissor lift), serial number range from GS6912-1300. Covers: complete GSDS fault code list (272 DTCs + 6 OIC), hydraulic schematic, electrical wiring diagrams, drive system diagnosis, platform leveling, CAN bus troubleshooting. The GS-2669 DC is a rough terrain scissor with foam-filled tires, 4WD, 9m working height. Key fault codes: code 18 (pothole guard), codes 42-47 (coil errors), E4 (low battery), E6 (tilt). Service manual includes fault retrieval procedure and corrective actions for all codes.

title: Manitou 160 ATJ 180 ATJ Operator Manual PDF | Savehyr.se
content_summary: Operator manual covering Manitou 160 ATJ and 180 ATJ articulating boom lifts. The 180 ATJ has 18.18m working height, Deutz TCD 3.6 diesel engine, 230kg platform capacity. Manual covers: fault indicator lights and meanings, emergency procedures (emergency descent from platform), daily pre-operation checks, maintenance schedule (250h/500h/1000h). Safety system includes: overload sensor, tilt sensor, emergency E-stop, platform leveling system. Diagnostic access via iV diagnostic interface (Manitou-specific tool). For engine fault codes: Deutz EMR3/MD1 system with SPN/FMI format. Platform-level fault codes accessed via main display and iV tool. Emergency descent: open manual descent valve at base, lower platform slowly.

title: Skyjack SJ4732 DC Electric Scissors Service Manual 223198ABA August 2020 | Rentalex
content_summary: Full service manual for Skyjack SJ4732 DC electric scissor lift, document 223198ABA August 2020 (ANSI/CSA, CE, AS, KC standards). Covers: LED error code quick reference chart, GP module diagnostics (GP102 controller), fault code reading procedure (LED flash sequences), drive system troubleshooting, hydraulic system diagnosis, battery charger fault codes (BCF). Key fault codes: OVRLOAD (platform overloaded — check load cell and wiring), TILT (slope sensor active — move to level ground), DRIVE FAULT (drive system error — check motor, drive contactor, wiring harness), ARMGUARD (arm guard activated), HWFS STALL D (hydraulic stall during drive). Reset: cycle key switch and check ground control/platform control. The SJ4732 is a 14m rough-terrain scissor with 4WD and oscillating axle.

title: Skyjack Tech Tip December 2024 — RT Scissor GP Modules | Skyjack Official
content_summary: Official Skyjack Tech Tip December 2024 covering RT (Rough Terrain) scissor lift GP Module diagnostics. Addresses: GP module fault code reading procedure for R/T scissors (GP102 and variants), LED flash code sequence interpretation, common GP module failure modes, reset procedures, and when to replace vs. reprogram the GP module. GP modules on Skyjack RT scissors (SJ6832 RT, SJ4732 RT, SJ8841 RT) use same LED-flash fault code architecture as DC scissors but with terrain-specific codes. Module identification: all GP modules have similar operation but fault code lists differ by model — always reference serial-number-specific service manual. Tech tip also covers replacement part numbers for GP modules on current R/T scissor range.

title: J1939-73 Diagnostics Explained — DM1, DTCs, SPN, FMI | CSS Electronics
content_summary: Technical reference for SAE J1939-73 diagnostic messaging protocol used in all heavy AWP equipment (JLG diesel booms, Manitou ATJ, Haulotte diesel, Genie S/Z diesel booms, Skyjack diesel scissors). Explains: DM1 (Diagnostic Message 1) — active fault broadcast on J1939 CAN bus, SPN (Suspect Parameter Number) identifies the faulty component (SPN 100 = oil pressure, SPN 110 = coolant temp, SPN 190 = engine speed, SPN 91 = throttle, SPN 636 = crankshaft sensor), FMI (Failure Mode Identifier) identifies fault type (FMI 0 = above normal, FMI 1 = below normal, FMI 2 = erratic, FMI 3 = short to +, FMI 4 = short to ground, FMI 5 = open circuit, FMI 12 = bad device). Lamp status in DM1: Red Stop Lamp, Yellow Warning Lamp, MIL, protect lamp. Cross-brand reference for all Deutz-powered AWP machines.

title: Terex Tech Tip #87 — Troubleshooting J1939 Connector | Terex Official
content_summary: Official Terex Tech Tip #87 for troubleshooting J1939 data link connectors on Terex/Genie equipment. J1939 is the CAN bus standard used on all Genie diesel boom lifts (S-40, S-45, S-60, S-65, S-80, Z-45, Z-60) and rough-terrain scissors. Covers: J1939 network topology (two 120Ω termination resistors required), three-wire connector (yellow = CAN-H, green = CAN-L, shield = ground), wiring fault diagnosis (open circuit, short circuit to ground or battery+, intermittent connection), connector inspection and replacement procedure. Diagnostic steps: (1) disconnect all ECUs except two, measure resistance across J1939 lines — should read ~60Ω (two 120Ω in parallel); (2) wiggle harness to find intermittent faults; (3) check connector pins for corrosion/damage. Key symptom: CAN bus faults manifest as multiple simultaneous ECU DTCs across engine, platform, and chassis modules.

title: Dingli JCPT0607A Mobile Scissor Lift Operator's Manual | JH Lift Korea
content_summary: Operator's manual for Dingli JCPT0607A mobile scissor lift (6m working height, 600kg capacity), distributed by Korean dealer JH Lift. Contains: complete fault code list with LED display codes (2-digit and 3-digit), operating procedures, safety requirements, maintenance schedule. Fault code section covers: Code 10 (GENERAL ALARM ECU), Code 20 (GENERAL ALARM TM1 — traction motor controller fault), Code 30 (GENERAL ALARM PCU), Code 31 (PCU CPU0 FAULT), Code 32 (PCU CPU1 FAULT), Code 54 (PRESS SENSOR ERROR), Code 57 (OVERLOAD), Code 58 (POTHOLE protection activated), Code 59 (HIGH POSITION LIMIT), Code 69 (power relay sleep / low battery), Code 110 (ECU WATCHDOG), Code 111-112 (CAN bus timeout). Reset procedure: cycle Emergency Stop button. The JCPT0607A uses DL-00000693 controller platform common to 0607/1208/1412 DC series.

title: Snorkel All Models — Repair Parts, Maintenance, Operators Manuals | MHH Auto Forum
content_summary: MHH Auto forum thread aggregating Snorkel lift manuals for all models. Covers: S-series scissors (S1930, S2030, S2646, S3219E, S3246, S3247), SL-series (SL20, SL26, SL30), A-series articulating booms (A38E, A46JE, A46JE+), TB-series telescopic booms (TB42, TB50), SB-series straight booms (SB10J, SB15J, SB30J). Includes repair parts manuals, maintenance schedules, and operators manuals. Fault code architecture across Snorkel range: codes 01-39 are resettable (press E-stop button); codes 51-69 require component replacement (solenoid coil, sensor, or module). Common codes: NEUTRAL FAULT (drive controller sees non-neutral input at startup — joystick not centered), DRIVE FAULT (drive system error), code 18 (pothole guard). Solenoid coil test: 20-30Ω resistance.

title: Snorkel Technical Manuals & Service Bulletins | Snorkel New Zealand Official
content_summary: Official Snorkel New Zealand support page providing access to technical manuals and service bulletins for all Snorkel products. The NZ portal covers models including SB boom lifts (SB10J, SB15J, SB30J) that may not be listed on the global Snorkel support site. Service bulletins address: solenoid valve replacement procedures, EMS (Electronic Management System) software updates, battery charger upgrades, pothole bar adjustment procedures. Technical manuals include operator, parts, and service documentation. The SB-series straight booms use the Snorkel EMS control system with the same fault code architecture (01-39 resettable, 51-69 replace) as the S and A series. Key SB boom fault codes: neutral fault (joystick not at center before startup), overload (platform load exceeds rated), tilt fault (exceed safe slope).
equipment_model: Snorkel SB boom lifts (SB10J, SB15J, SB30J)

title: Skyjack SJ45AJ Technical Publication Document #7040 | Skyjack TechPub Official
content_summary: Official Skyjack Technical Publications document #7040 for the SJ45AJ articulating boom lift. The SJ45AJ is a 14.2m working height diesel articulating boom lift using the GP102 controller. Document covers: GP102 fault code system (LED flash sequences — each code displayed as 2 groups of flashes separated by pause), LED error code quick reference chart, drive fault diagnosis, hydraulic system troubleshooting, control system calibration. Common fault codes on SJ45AJ: OVRLOAD (overload sensor active), TILT (tilt sensor — level machine), DRIVE FAULT (drive system fault — check drive motor, contactor, wiring), ARMGUARD (platform arm guard activated), HWFS STALL D (hydraulic stall during drive function). Key diagnostic note: GP102 reports only one fault at a time. Reset: cycle key switch. If fault persists, use Skyjack authorized service center and reference serial-number-specific manual.

title: Skyjack SJ8841 RT Technical Publication Document #7353 | Skyjack TechPub Official
content_summary: Official Skyjack Technical Publications document #7353 for the SJ8841 RT (Rough Terrain) scissor lift. The SJ8841 RT is a large rough-terrain scissor with 14.8m working height and 680kg capacity. Document covers: GP module LED error codes, diagnostic procedures, hydraulic system (diesel-hydraulic drive), fault reading via LED flash sequences. The SJ8841 RT uses the same GP module family as other RT scissors (SJ4732, SJ6832). Key fault codes: OVRLOAD, TILT, DRIVE FAULT, HWFS STALL D, ARMGUARD, BCF (battery charger fault) codes. Rough terrain specific: oscillating axle diagnostics, 4WD troubleshooting, differential lock fault. Maintenance: hydraulic oil service (every 500h), drive chain tension check, oscillating axle bearings.

title: Snorkel Scissor & Boom Lifts Manuals — Operator, Repair, Wiring, Fault Codes | PDFManual4Trucks
content_summary: Collection page for Snorkel scissor lift and boom lift PDF manuals including: operator's manuals, repair/service manuals, parts catalogs, electrical wiring diagrams, and error code references. Covers Snorkel S-series scissors (S1930, S3219E, S3246, S3247), SL-series (SL26, SL30), A-series articulating booms (A38E, A46JE), TB-series telescopic booms, and SB-series straight booms (SB10J, SB15J, SB30J). Fault code architecture: codes 01-39 resettable via E-stop, codes 51-69 require component replacement. Wiring diagrams cover: control module, drive system, hydraulic system, battery/charger. Useful cross-reference for Snorkel and Upright (same parent company — Time Manufacturing) which share similar control architectures.
equipment_model: Snorkel all models incl. SB boom lifts

title: Manitou Service Manuals & Wiring Diagrams All Models | MHH Auto Forum
content_summary: MHH Auto forum thread aggregating Manitou service manuals and wiring diagrams for all models including Manitou ATJ articulating boom lifts (160 ATJ, 180 ATJ, 200 ATJ, 260 ATJ). Includes links to: 180 ATJ Euro 3 electrical schematic (UPC30 control unit diagram), Manitou diagnostic system iV tool documentation, telehandler (MT/MLT series) service manuals with SPN/FMI fault codes. For the 180 ATJ specifically: Deutz TCD 3.6 L4 engine fault codes (accessed via iV diagnostic tool), platform control module fault indicators, hydraulic system schematic (boom, swing, jib, leveling). Key fault indicators: overload sensor circuit, tilt sensor (jib leveling), emergency descent procedure via manual valve. ATJ260 (26m) uses same UPC30 platform but with SPN/FMI Deutz Stage IV engine codes.
